senses.definition: a place where children learn things from teachers

examples.x: He forgot his homework and had to rush back to school to get it.
My children go to school to learn new things and make friends.
She takes the bus to school every morning.
We have a science project due next week at school.
We study different subjects like math, science, and English at school.

senses.definition: an institution that offers higher education or specialized trainings for different professions

examples.x: I am excited to start college and pursue my degree.
She attends college to study business administration.
They are preparing for their final exams at college.
We have access to a library with extensive resources at college.
We have to write a research paper for our college class.

senses.definition: an educational institution at the highest level, where we can study for a degree or do research

examples.x: I will graduate from university next year with a degree in psychology.
She attends a prestigious university known for its engineering program.
She received a scholarship to help fund her university education.
We have a university lecture on art history this afternoon.
We have access to a state-of-the-art library at the university.

senses.definition: a place that children under the age of six can go to learn and play

examples.x: He made new friends at preschool and enjoys sharing toys with them.
I volunteer at the preschool to help with art activities and storytime.
She is learning numbers and letters at preschool to prepare for kindergarten.
We drop off our son at preschool in the morning and pick him up in the afternoon.

senses.definition: a room that students are taught in, particularly in a college, school, or university

examples.x: I keep my school supplies organized in my backpack for the classroom.
She enjoys decorating the classroom with colorful artwork.
The classroom is filled with desks, chairs, and a chalkboard.
They work on group projects together in the classroom.
We have a class discussion in the classroom to share our ideas.

senses.definition: a person who is studying at a school, university, or college

examples.x: Can you introduce yourself to the new student in class?
She studies diligently to prepare for her exams as a student.
She takes notes during lectures to review later as a dedicated student.
The student raises their hand to answer the teacher's question.
They collaborate with other students on group projects.

senses.definition: a set of printed pages that are held together in a cover so that we can turn them and read them

examples.x: I always carry a book in my bag so I can read during my commute or whenever I have free time.
I love reading books; they transport me to different worlds and ignite my imagination.
My favorite book is a classic novel that has been passed down through generations.
She placed the book on the shelf and admired its colorful cover.
The librarian helped me find a book on ancient history for my research project.

senses.definition: a small book with plain or ruled pages that we can write or draw in

examples.x: He writes down his ideas and thoughts in his notebook.
My kids personalize their notebooks with stickers and drawings.
She uses her notebook to keep track of her daily to-do list.
They exchange notes with classmates by borrowing each other's notebooks.
We use our notebooks to practice writing and improve our handwriting skills.

senses.definition: something made of leather, cloth, plastic, or paper that we use to carry things in, particularly when we are traveling or shopping

examples.x: Can you hold my bag while I tie my shoelaces?
He carries a gym bag with his workout clothes and water bottle.
I pack my lunch in a small bag before leaving for work.
She packed her swimming gear in a waterproof bag for the pool.

senses.definition: an instrument for writing or drawing with ink, usually made of plastic or metal

examples.x: Can you lend me your red pen to fill out this form?
He writes his thoughts and ideas in a journal with a fancy pen.
She uses a black pen to sign important documents.
They keep a collection of colorful pens for drawing and doodling.
We sign our names with a pen when writing greeting cards.

senses.definition: a tool with a slim piece of wood and a thin, colored part in the middle, that we use for writing or drawing

examples.x: He uses a pencil to shade and create different tones in his artwork.
I use a pencil to sketch and draw.
She lends her pencil to a classmate who forgot to bring one.
She sharpens her pencil before starting her math homework.
We mark important passages in a book with a pencil underline.

senses.definition: a small tool used for removing the marks of a pencil from a piece of paper

examples.x: He gently rubs the eraser over the words to erase them completely.
He lost his eraser and had to ask his friend to borrow one.
I use an eraser to correct my mistakes when writing.
She shares her eraser with her classmates during exams.
They keep a small eraser in their pencil case for quick corrections.

senses.definition: a type of pen that has a thick tip

examples.x: I keep a set of markers in my art supplies for various art projects.
I use a black marker to write my name on my belongings.
They use washable markers for coloring books and crafts.

senses.definition: schoolwork that students have to do at home

examples.x: I always double-check my homework to ensure it is accurate.
I have a lot of homework to complete tonight.
My daughter spends a few hours every evening doing her homework.
They need to finish their English homework before the deadline.
We use textbooks and online resources to help us with our homework.

senses.definition: the study of past events, especially as a subject in school or university

examples.x: Can you recommend any online resources for studying world history?
I enjoy studying history to learn about significant events that shaped our world.
She wants to pursue a degree in history and become a historian.
We have a history exam next week, so I need to study.
We study the history of our country in social studies class.

senses.definition: the system of communication by spoken or written words, that the people of a particular country or region use

examples.x: He wants to become bilingual and speak multiple languages fluently.
Learning a new language opens doors to different cultures and opportunities.
She practices speaking the language with native speakers to improve her fluency.
She takes language classes to enhance her communication skills.
They use online resources to study grammar and vocabulary in the language.

senses.definition: knowledge about the structure and behavior of the natural and physical world, especially based on testing and proving facts

examples.x: I enjoy conducting experiments and making observations in science class.
I find it fascinating to learn about the wonders of the natural world through science.
She learns about the laws of physics and the properties of matter in her science lessons.
We discuss current scientific advancements and discoveries in science class.
We explore the different branches of science, such as chemistry and astronomy.

senses.definition: students as a whole that are taught together

examples.x: The teacher greeted the class as they entered the classroom, ready to begin the day's lesson.
The class participated eagerly in the group discussion, sharing their diverse perspectives on the topic.
Every Friday, the class gathers for a weekly quiz to test their understanding of the material covered during the week.
The class enjoyed a field trip to the museum, where they explored exhibits related to their history curriculum.
The class elected a representative to voice their concerns and suggestions during student council meetings.
